findings.evidence: Mathlib/RingTheory/Bialgebra/TensorProduct.lean:73-75 defines map under [Bialgebra S C] [Bialgebra R D]; Mathlib/RingTheory/Coalgebra/TensorProduct.lean:69 proves counit_tmul under [Coalgebra R B] [Coalgebra S A]. No lemma in this file uses the antipode or Hopf-specific structure.
findings.fix: Relax the variable block to [Bialgebra k A] [Bialgebra k B] [Bialgebra k C] (which covers all lemmas here), or split into coalgebra-only and bialgebra blocks for the respective declarations.
findings.issue: Shared variable block requires [HopfAlgebra k A] [HopfAlgebra k B] [HopfAlgebra k C], but baseChangeMap and its functoriality lemmas use only bialgebra structure, and baseChange_counit_tmul uses only coalgebra structure. Mathlib's Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map uses [Bialgebra S C] [Bialgebra R D] and Coalgebra.TensorProduct.counit_tmul uses [Coalgebra R B] [Coalgebra S A].

findings.evidence: Line 60: baseChangeMap f (x ⊗ₜ a) = x ⊗ₜ f a. Substitute x := (1 : K) gives exactly baseChangeMap_one_tmul's conclusion on line 95.
findings.fix: Remove baseChangeMap_one_tmul (lines 91-96). The consumer can use baseChangeMap_tmul with x := 1.
findings.issue: baseChangeMap_one_tmul is the special case x=1 of baseChangeMap_tmul (line 60) and is fully redundant — it re-proves nothing new and adds a duplicate @[simp] lemma.

summary: The variable block over-assumes HopfAlgebra for all declarations when baseChangeMap and its lemmas need only Bialgebra, and baseChange_counit_tmul needs only Coalgebra. Additionally, baseChangeMap_one_tmul is a redundant special case of baseChangeMap_tmul.
